commit_message:
Use `systemd-modules-load` in place of custom driver-loading unit Use `systemd-modules-load` in place of custom driver-loading unit **NOTE:** Merge only after #13!

This is here now, in case we want to merge it at some point!

Setup is pretty trivial — the only really new thing here is the need for a `make modules_install` invocation. I've added a Makefile target for that, and it's documented in the associated README as well.

If there's anything else that should go along with this (especial

examples.commit_message:
Use `systemd-modules-load` in place of custom driver-loading unit Use `systemd-modules-load` in place of custom driver-loading unit **NOTE:** Merge only after #13!

This is here now, in case we want to merge it at some point!

Setup is pretty trivial — the only really new thing here is the need for a `make modules_install` invocation. I've added a Makefile target for that, and it's documented in the associated README as well.

If there's anything else that should go along with this (especial
examples.patch:
@@ -1,9 +1,19 @@
+# Set default options
 KDIR ?= ../linux-socfpga
-default:
-	$(MAKE) -C $(KDIR) ARCH=arm M=$(CURDIR) CROSS_COMPILE=arm-linux-gnueabihf-
+ROOTFS ?= /srv/nfs/de10nano/ubuntu-rootfs
+
+# Compose our full "make" command, with relevant options
+MAKECMD = $(MAKE) -C $(KDIR) M=$(CURDIR) ARCH=arm CROSS_COMPILE=arm-linux-gnueabihf-
+
+# This is the default if no target is specified
+modules:
+	$(MAKECMD) modules
+
+modules_install:
+	$(MAKECMD) INSTALL_MOD_PATH=$(ROOTFS) modules_install
 
 clean:
-	$(MAKE) -C $(KDIR) ARCH=arm M=$(CURDIR) clean
+	$(MAKECMD) clean
 
 help:
-	$(MAKE) -C $(KDIR) ARCH=arm M=$(CURDIR) help
+	$(MAKECMD) help
examples.before_code:
KDIR ?= ../linux-socfpga
default:
	$(MAKE) -C $(KDIR) ARCH=arm M=$(CURDIR) CROSS_COMPILE=arm-linux-gnueabihf-

clean:
	$(MAKE) -C $(KDIR) ARCH=arm M=$(CURDIR) clean

help:
	$(MAKE) -C $(KDIR) ARCH=arm M=$(CURDIR) help
examples.after_code:
# Set default options
KDIR ?= ../linux-socfpga
ROOTFS ?= /srv/nfs/de10nano/ubuntu-rootfs

# Compose our full "make" command, with relevant options
MAKECMD = $(MAKE) -C $(KDIR) M=$(CURDIR) ARCH=arm CROSS_COMPILE=arm-linux-gnueabihf-

# This is the default if no target is specified
modules:
	$(MAKECMD) modules

modules_install:
	$(MAKECMD) INSTALL_MOD_PATH=$(ROOTFS) modules_install

clean:
	$(MAKECMD) clean

help:
	$(MAKECMD) help
